feedback.issue: R19 and the requirements' restart-output assumption require a completed 'Repeat from the Beginning' restart to show its fresh, replaced results inline in the paused view before the person chooses their next action. A restart re-runs the entire Define DAG and regenerates business_requirements (Define's headline deliverable, produced by Requirements Writer, step 2) alongside discovery_notes and open_questions, but the design surfaces only discovery_notes (new define_discovery_notes helper, §6.2) and open_questions (existing block, §6.3) inline — the R19 traceability row itself states fresh output is shown 'via R9/R11's same inline blocks'. Per discovery-notes F1, business_requirements is surfaced nowhere in the UI today and would need net-new surfacing, which the design does not add. Result: after a restart the person lands on the paused menu and is asked to decide Done (R20/R21) without ever seeing the regenerated requirements inline. Raised in iteration 1; not addressed or rebutted in this design.

R13/R14's 'Ask Human' action is implemented purely as an anchor (#define-ask-human) that scrolls to the existing open-questions/answer form, which renders only when questions.present?. When a paused Define currently has no open_questions (e.g. paused immediately after Explore or Requirements Writer, before the conditional Clarifying-Questions step has run — the region the ask calls out as primary), the Ask Human button targets a non-existent anchor and no answer form is shown, so R14's free-form notes have no submission path in that state. The requirements-completeness critic explicitly deferred this empty-state to the design phase; the design does not resolve it. Raised in iteration 1; not addressed or rebutted in this design.
